stories.title: What to watch for after Jensen Huang’s Japan visit
stories.simple_summary: The head of a major AI chip company visited Japan and made business deals with Japanese technology companies.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and tech companies — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Can an Apple lawsuit derail OpenAI’s hardware plans?
stories.simple_summary: Apple is suing OpenAI, and people are wondering if this lawsuit will stop OpenAI from making AI devices and becoming a public company (selling stock to regular people).
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly a business battle between big companies — you don't need to do anything about it right now.

stories.title: I hate that I don’t hate this song made with Suno
stories.simple_summary: A music critic was surprised to find that a song made by AI (using a tool called Suno) was actually pretty good, even though they usually think AI-made music is boring.
stories.why_it_matters: AI music-making tools are getting better, so if you enjoy music, you might hear more of it in the future — but you don't have to use these tools yourself.

stories.title: ‘Odyssey’ director Christopher Nolan calls AI an obvious ‘Trojan horse’
stories.simple_summary: Famous movie director Christopher Nolan said that AI is a 'Trojan horse' — meaning it looks harmless but has hidden dangers inside.
stories.why_it_matters: A respected filmmaker is warning about AI risks, which adds to an important conversation about AI safety — but there's no specific action you need to take.

stories.title: Nonprofit Current AI is racing to build the World Wide Web of AI, free for all
stories.simple_summary: A nonprofit organization is working to build AI tools that anyone in the world can use for free, no matter what country or culture they're from.
stories.why_it_matters: This could make AI tools more available to small businesses and people who can't afford expensive software — this is genuinely good news.

stories.title: Dave Eggers told OpenAI staff  that ChatGPT was ‘silencing an entire generation’
stories.simple_summary: Author Dave Eggers told OpenAI employees that AI tools like ChatGPT are hurting writers by making it easier to create writing without human writers doing the work.
stories.why_it_matters: If you're a writer or work with writing, AI tools that generate text could affect your work — this is something worth paying attention to.

stories.title: Kimi: Threat or menace?
stories.simple_summary: A Chinese AI company released a new version of its AI chatbot, which some people worry about because China might use AI differently than American companies do.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors worried about global competition — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: The apps, gadgets, and tools every reader needs
stories.simple_summary: This is a recommendation column about useful apps, gadgets, and tools that readers might find helpful.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a shopping guide, not news — only relevant if you're looking for product recommendations.

stories.title: Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out
stories.simple_summary: A venture capitalist (investor) predicts that the huge amount of money being made from AI will eventually be shared with more people in society.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly about how wealth might be distributed — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Vertu wants executives to pay $6,880 for an AI agent — here’s how it actually performs
stories.simple_summary: A luxury phone company is selling an expensive AI assistant (a tool that helps you with tasks) for $6,880, and a reviewer tested how well it actually works.
stories.why_it_matters: Unless you're considering buying a very expensive phone with AI features, this is just a product review — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
stories.simple_summary: Databricks, a company that helps other companies use AI, is now worth $188 billion and is becoming very successful in the AI business.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: The Zoom hack that says, ‘Don’t record me’
stories.simple_summary: Zoom is adding a feature that lets people say 'don't record me,' but the article questions whether recording and summarizing every meeting is even a good idea.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Zoom for business meetings, this is worth knowing about — you may soon have more control over whether your meetings get recorded and turned into summaries.
